hints.In: 'In' is a preposition, not a verb
hints.principio: 'principio' is a noun in the ablative case
hints.Verbum: 'Verbum' is a noun meaning 'word' — it's the subject

hints.Present: Present of 'esse' would be 'est' — 'erat' has the -ba- imperfect marker
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'erat' uses the imperfect stem
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect of 'esse' would be 'fuerat' — 'erat' is simpler

hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love), a 1st conjugation verb
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry), an irregular verb
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the principal parts of 'ire' (to go)

hints.In: 'In' is a preposition — it can't be the subject
hints.erat: 'erat' is the verb, not the subject

hints.Conjunction: Conjunctions join clauses (et, sed) — 'In' governs a noun
hints.Interjection: Interjections are exclamations — 'In' takes an object

hints.Accusative: 'in' + accusative means motion toward — this is location, not motion

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'principium' would be 'principium' — the -o ending is ablative
hints.Dative: Dative of 'principium' would be 'principio' too, but 'in' governs ablative

hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — 'principium' ends in -um, which is 2nd

translation: In the beginning was the Word
